steps.action: Inspect source import patterns in src/components/graph/GraphExplorer.tsx, src/components/graph/LazyGraphExplorer.tsx, and src/app/page.tsx.
steps.expected: New surfaces use dynamic() / lazy wrappers instead of static imports in the initial page path.
steps.observed: GraphExplorer dynamically imports MacroThesisSurface, HftEdgeSurface, LlmProductSurface, and TruthPerpFlywheelSurface; page.tsx renders LazyGraphExplorer and LazyProofModule through client-boundary wrappers, and LazyGraphExplorer uses next/dynamic with ssr:false.

steps.action: Run npm run build and inspect .next/diagnostics/route-bundle-stats.json plus emitted chunk files.
steps.expected: New surface implementations are split out of the / route first-load chunk list so they do not ship in the initial bundle.
steps.observed: The / route first-load chunk list contains 10 chunk files and excludes the four content-depth surface chunks. The emitted surface chunks were 0lft90ju3r~q1.js (Macro Thesis, 7121 bytes), 0k1n41qlqs0n-.js (HFT Edge, 6721 bytes), 0an849snf-a6r.js (LLM Product, 5824 bytes), and 05vsxht91loj0.js (TRUTH-PERP & Flywheel, 7206 bytes), all absent from firstLoadChunkPaths.

steps.action: Review build output for direct initial-bundle size evidence.
steps.expected: Build output exposes bundle-impact information for the initial route.
steps.observed: Next.js diagnostics reported firstLoadUncompressedJsBytes=671528 for / and chunk membership, but did not emit a direct gzipped before/after delta; first-load exclusion was used as the concrete bundle-impact signal.

evidence.sourceInspection: GraphExplorer.tsx lines 44-71 show next/dynamic imports for all four new surfaces; LazyGraphExplorer.tsx lines 20-26 show the graph shell behind dynamic(..., { ssr:false }); page.tsx lines 36-55 show lazy wrappers used in the app entry path.
evidence.buildImpact: All four new surface chunks were emitted separately and excluded from the / route first-load chunk list according to .next/diagnostics/route-bundle-stats.json and chunk inspection.

assertions.title: GitHub push successful

steps.expected: Release-ready state shows a clean working tree.
steps.observed: git status reported ## main...origin/main [ahead 6] and one modified tracked file: .factory/library/user-testing.md.

steps.expected: HEAD is synchronized with origin/main (0	0) for a completed push state.
steps.observed: rev-list returned 0	6, meaning HEAD is 6 commits ahead of the local origin/main tracking ref and not synchronized.

steps.action: Capture exact local/remote commit evidence with git log -1 --oneline --decorate HEAD and origin/main.
steps.expected: HEAD and origin/main point to the same release commit.
steps.observed: HEAD is 45ab584 (validation: record polish-release scrutiny rerun pass) while origin/main is 5e31591 (polish: remove hardcoded Vercel metadata alias).

assertions.issues: Working tree is not clean and HEAD is ahead of origin/main by 6 commits, so the repo does not satisfy a clean-and-pushed release state.

steps.expected: Build exits 0 with zero errors or warnings.
steps.observed: Exit code 0. Next.js 16.2.1/Turbopack compiled successfully, completed TypeScript, generated static pages, and the log contained no warning/error lines.

steps.expected: TypeScript exits 0 with zero errors or warnings.
steps.observed: Exit code 0 with an empty log file, indicating no reported diagnostics.

steps.expected: ESLint exits 0 with zero errors or warnings.
steps.observed: Exit code 0; the log only showed the npm script preamble and eslint invocation, with no warning/error lines.

frictions.description: The Next.js/Turbopack build output did not emit a direct gzipped before/after delta for the initial bundle.
frictions.resolution: Used .next/diagnostics/route-bundle-stats.json plus emitted chunk inspection to confirm the new surfaces are split into separate chunks and excluded from the / route first-load chunk list.

summary: Tested 3 assertions: 2 passed and 1 failed. VAL-POLISH-002 passed on source/build chunk-splitting evidence, VAL-POLISH-003 failed because the working tree is dirty and main is ahead of origin/main by 6 commits, and VAL-POLISH-004 passed with npm run build, npx tsc --noEmit, and npm run lint all exiting 0 without warning output.
